proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal feature_057 (range 0-2; distribution suggestive of an ECOG-like performance status) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = sm.add_constant(df[['feature_057']].astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic regression of objective_response on feature_057: response rate falls monotonically from 21.3% at value 0 (n=17,592) to 15.5% at value 1 (n=24,971) to 11.2% at value 2 (n=7,437). Logit coefficient -0.384 (OR 0.681 per unit), p=1.8e-97. Strong inverse relationship.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035 = 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_035 = 0 (binary feature, prevalence ~7%).

analyses.code:

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 22.1% with feature_035=1 (n=3,624) vs 16.5% with feature_035=0 (n=46,376). Logit coefficient +0.364 (OR 1.44), p=4.1e-18. Highly significant positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature_011 (range 0-23.6, right-skewed) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:

analyses.result_summary: Logit coefficient -0.0401 per unit (OR 0.961), p=7.4e-35. Patients with feature_011 above the median have a response rate of 15.2% vs 18.6% for those at or below the median. Strong negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_006 (continuous, range 20-100; distribution consistent with a percentage-like metric such as a marrow blast or marker percentage) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:

X = sm.add_constant(df[['feature_006']].astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coefficient -0.00567 per unit (OR 0.994), p=1.2e-19. Modest but highly significant inverse relationship: a 30-unit increase in feature_006 corresponds to roughly a 16% relative decrease in odds of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5, near-Gaussian, distribution consistent with serum albumin in g/dL) are associated with a higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:

analyses.result_summary: Logit coefficient +0.115 per unit (OR 1.12), p=1.4e-6. Patients with feature_099 >= 4.0 had a response rate of 17.6% versus 16.4% in those below 4.0 (consistent with higher 'albumin-like' values predicting better response).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_092 (continuous, range 0.5-500, very right-skewed; distribution consistent with a leukocyte/lab count such as WBC) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_063 (continuous, range 0.03-300, very right-skewed; consistent with a serum lab value such as LDH or beta2-microglobulin) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_093 = 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_093 = 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_014 = 1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_014 = 0 (binary feature, prevalence ~40%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older feature_078 (continuous, range 18-92, mean 68; distribution consistent with patient age in years) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = sm.add_constant(df[['feature_078']].astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coefficient +0.00040 per year (OR 1.0004), p=0.72. Response rates by decade are essentially flat (16.7% <60, 17.2% 60-70, 16.7% 70-80, 16.8% 80+). No detectable main effect of age on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rate differs by self-reported race in feature_005, with white patients having a higher unadjusted response rate than non-white patients.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
pd.crosstab(df['feature_005'], df['objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response rates: white 17.3% (n=32,498), hispanic 16.7% (n=7,631), asian 16.3% (n=2,970), black 15.7% (n=5,889), other 14.7% (n=1,012). 5x2 chi-square p=0.0096; white vs non-white chi-square p=0.0021 (white +1.1 absolute percentage points). Statistically significant racial gradient at the unadjusted level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rate differs by insurance status (feature_087) across categories medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
pd.crosstab(df['feature_087'], df['objective_response'])
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: medicare 17.2%, medicaid 16.9%, private 16.7%, uninsured 16.6%. 4x2 chi-square p=0.54. No detectable main effect of insurance category on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression that includes feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_092, feature_099, feature_063, feature_035, and feature_078, each clinical variable retains an independent association with objective_response.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
feats=['feature_057','feature_011','feature_006','feature_092','feature_099','feature_063','feature_035','feature_078']
X = sm.add_constant(df[feats].astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs: feature_057 0.682 (p=1.3e-96), feature_035 1.43 (p=1.9e-17), feature_011 0.961 per unit (p=1.3e-34), feature_006 0.994 per unit (p=7.0e-20), feature_099 1.13 per unit (p=9.3e-7), feature_092 0.998 per unit (p~1e-5), feature_063 0.993 per unit (p~2e-6), feature_078 (age) NS (p=0.75). All clinical predictors except age remain independently and highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unadjusted disparity in objective response rate by race (white > non-white) persists after adjustment for the clinical predictors in h14, indicating a residual race effect not mediated by measured prognostic features.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
feats=['feature_057','feature_011','feature_006','feature_092','feature_099','feature_063','feature_035','feature_078']
race = pd.get_dummies(df['feature_005'], prefix='race', drop_first=True).astype(int)
X = sm.add_constant(pd.concat([df[feats], race], axis=1).astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for clinical predictors, race coefficients (reference: asian) are: white +0.079 (p=0.13), hispanic +0.044 (p=0.46), black -0.030 (p=0.62), other -0.117 (p=0.25). Joint Wald test of race terms is not significant. The unadjusted disparity is largely explained by differences in clinical variables across racial groups; hypothesis of a residual race effect is NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance status (feature_087) is independently associated with objective_response after adjustment for the clinical predictors in h14.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
feats=['feature_057','feature_011','feature_006','feature_092','feature_099','feature_063','feature_035','feature_078']
ins = pd.get_dummies(df['feature_087'], prefix='ins', drop_first=True).astype(int)
X = sm.add_constant(pd.concat([df[feats], ins], axis=1).astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: After clinical adjustment, insurance dummy coefficients (reference: medicaid) are: medicare +0.022 (p=0.57), private -0.020 (p=0.59), uninsured -0.010 (p=0.88). All non-significant. No independent association with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_035 = 1 on objective_response is modified by performance-status-like feature_057 — specifically, the absolute response benefit of feature_035 is larger in patients with low feature_057 (multiplicative interaction on the logit scale).

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = pd.DataFrame({'a':df['feature_035'],'b':df['feature_057']})
X['ab']=X['a']*X['b']
X = sm.add_constant(X.astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction coefficient feature_035 x feature_057 = +0.027, p=0.67. Stratum-specific absolute differences (RR1 - RR0) are 6.2pp at PS=0, 5.4pp at PS=1, 4.4pp at PS=2 (slight attenuation but not statistically significant). On the multiplicative (log-odds) scale the effect of feature_035 is essentially uniform across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_011 on objective_response interacts with feature_057 — i.e., the per-unit log-odds penalty for higher feature_011 differs across performance-status-like strata.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = pd.DataFrame({'a':df['feature_011'],'b':df['feature_057']})
X['ab']=X['a']*X['b']
X = sm.add_constant(X.astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction coefficient feature_011 x feature_057 = -0.0019, p=0.71. No evidence of effect-modification of feature_011 by feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_057 on objective_response is amplified in older patients (multiplicative interaction with feature_078, age in years).

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = pd.DataFrame({'age':df['feature_078'],'ps':df['feature_057']})
X['inter']=X['age']*X['ps']
X = sm.add_constant(X.astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction age x feature_057 = +0.0022, p=0.19. Trend is in the direction opposite to the hypothesis (interaction positive, suggesting slight attenuation of the PS penalty in older patients), and is not significant. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of feature_035 on objective_response differs between white and non-white patients (interaction term race_white x feature_035 in a logistic model).

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
feats=['feature_057','feature_011','feature_006','feature_092','feature_099','feature_063','feature_035','feature_078']
df['white'] = (df['feature_005']=='white').astype(int)
X = df[feats+['white']].copy()
X['white_x_035'] = X['white']*X['feature_035']
X = sm.add_constant(X.astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction race_white x feature_035 = +0.134, p=0.14. Stratum-specific absolute differences: white 16.8% vs 23.3% (+6.5pp); non-white range 14.3-16.4% vs 19.5-20.5% (+4.0-5.4pp). Effect is somewhat larger in white patients but the multiplicative interaction is not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each stratum of feature_057 (0, 1, 2), the response rate in patients with feature_035 = 1 is higher than in patients with feature_035 = 0 (consistent direction of the feature_035 effect across performance-status strata).

analyses.code: df.groupby(['feature_057','feature_035'])['objective_response'].agg(['mean','count'])
analyses.result_summary: PS=0: 27.0% (f035=1, n=1331) vs 20.8% (f035=0, n=16261); PS=1: 20.5% vs 15.1% (n=1777 vs 23194); PS=2: 15.3% vs 10.9% (n=516 vs 6921). Direction of the f035 benefit is consistent across all three PS strata; absolute benefit decreases with worsening PS but stratum-specific Fisher tests are each highly significant (p<1e-5 for PS=0 and PS=1; p≈0.001 for PS=2).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association between feature_057 and objective_response is consistent across all five racial categories of feature_005 (no qualitative race-by-PS effect modification).

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
for r in df['feature_005'].unique():
    sub = df[df['feature_005']==r]
    X = sm.add_constant(sub[['feature_057']].astype(float))
    sm.Logit(sub['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Race-stratified univariate logits of objective_response on feature_057: white -0.377 (p<1e-50, n=32498); asian -0.381 (p<1e-6, n=2970); black -0.442 (p<1e-10, n=5889); hispanic -0.382 (p<1e-12, n=7631); other -0.304 (p=0.024, n=1012). All five strata show significant, similar-magnitude negative effects. Direction is universal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the multi-level integer feature_018 (range 0-10; possibly a count of comorbidities or treatment cycles) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = sm.add_constant(df[['feature_018']].astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coefficient +0.0042 per unit (OR 1.004), p=0.34. Response rates across values 0-10 are essentially flat (range 16.3-19.0% with no monotonic trend). No detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The summed count of '1' values across all 80 binary integer features in the dataset (a generic 'positive feature load') is associated with objective_response.

analyses.code:
binary_cols=[c for c in df.columns if df[c].dtype=='int64' and df[c].nunique()==2 and c!='objective_response']
df['n_bin']=df[binary_cols].sum(axis=1)
import statsmodels.api as sm
X = sm.add_constant(df[['n_bin']].astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Mean n_bin = 14.6 (SD 3.2, range 1-28). Logit coefficient +0.0035 per additional positive feature, p=0.34. No detectable main effect — supports the view that the response signal is concentrated in a small subset of biologically informative features rather than a generic feature-burden index.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final adjusted model containing feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_092, feature_099, feature_063, feature_035, feature_078, race (feature_005) and insurance (feature_087), the dominant independent predictors of objective_response are feature_057 (negative), feature_035 (positive), feature_011 (negative), feature_006 (negative), feature_099 (positive), feature_092 (negative), and feature_063 (negative); race and insurance contribute no independent signal.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
feats=['feature_078','feature_011','feature_006','feature_092','feature_099','feature_063','feature_035','feature_093','feature_121','feature_014','feature_057','feature_125']
race = pd.get_dummies(df['feature_005'], prefix='race', drop_first=True).astype(int)
ins  = pd.get_dummies(df['feature_087'], prefix='ins',  drop_first=True).astype(int)
X = sm.add_constant(pd.concat([df[feats], race, ins], axis=1).astype(float))
sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Final 19-covariate model (LL=-22284, pseudo-R^2 = 0.019, LLR p=1.1e-171). Independently significant predictors (p<0.05): feature_057 (z=-20.9), feature_035 (z=+8.5), feature_011 (z=-12.3), feature_006 (z=-9.1), feature_099 (z=+4.9), feature_063 (z=-4.8), feature_092 (z=-4.4), feature_093 (z=+2.9), feature_014 (z=+2.4), feature_121 (z=-2.0). Age (feature_078), feature_125, all four race dummies, and all three insurance dummies are non-significant after adjustment. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: clinical/biomarker-like features carry the entire signal; demographic/sociodemographic variables are non-contributory after adjustment.
